description: AI WhatsApp agent that collects maintenance requests from tenants via WhatsApp (Twilio BSP), uses Claude Haiku 4.5 for guided conversation, handles photos, and creates SEIDO interventions automatically.

userStories.title: Install twilio + DB migration (sessions + WhatsApp columns)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the twilio package installed and the DB schema extended with ai_whatsapp_sessions table + whatsapp columns on ai_phone_numbers, so the backend can persist WhatsApp conversation state.
acceptanceCriteria: twilio package installed in node_modules

Migration creates ai_whatsapp_sessions table with: id, team_id, phone_number_id, contact_phone, status (CHECK: active/completed/expired/failed), messages (JSONB), extracted_data (JSONB), identified_user_id, intervention_id, language, last_message_at, created_at, updated_at
ai_phone_numbers extended with: whatsapp_enabled (boolean default false), twilio_whatsapp_number (text), twilio_account_sid (text)
RLS enabled on ai_whatsapp_sessions (SELECT for admin/gestionnaire only, writes via service_role)
Indexes on (team_id, contact_phone) WHERE active, last_message_at WHERE active, (team_id, created_at DESC)
updated_at trigger reuses update_ai_phone_updated_at()
npm run lint passes
userStories.notes: Migration already applied (db push --linked). Types regenerated.

userStories.title: Twilio WhatsApp service (send, download, validate)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a thin service wrapping the Twilio SDK for sending WhatsApp messages, downloading media, and validating webhook signatures.
acceptanceCriteria: lib/services/domain/ai-whatsapp/twilio-whatsapp.service.ts exports sendWhatsAppMessage(from, to, body) returning message SID
Exports downloadMedia(mediaUrl) returning { buffer, contentType } with Basic auth
Exports validateWebhookSignature(url, params, signature) using twilio.validateRequest()
Lazy singleton Twilio client (not instantiated at import time)
lib/services/domain/ai-whatsapp/index.ts re-exports all
npm run lint passes

userStories.title: Webhook POST handler (receive, validate, route, defer)
userStories.description: As the system, I want the WhatsApp webhook POST handler to parse Twilio form data, validate the signature, identify the team, and defer processing to after().
acceptanceCriteria: app/api/webhooks/whatsapp/route.ts POST handler parses application/x-www-form-urlencoded (formData)
Validates Twilio signature via X-Twilio-Signature header + validateWebhookSignature()
Identifies team by matching 'To' number against ai_phone_numbers.twilio_whatsapp_number
Returns 200 with empty TwiML <Response></Response> immediately
Defers heavy work to after() which calls handleIncomingWhatsApp()
Returns 200 even on errors (Twilio retries on non-2xx)
Existing GET handler unchanged
npm run lint passes
userStories.notes: formData parser, Twilio signature validation, after() defer, console→logger in GET

userStories.title: Conversation engine (Claude AI + sessions + photos)
userStories.description: As a tenant messaging on WhatsApp, I want the AI to manage a guided 4-step conversation (identify, describe, confirm, close), handle my photos via Claude vision, and persist the session state.
acceptanceCriteria: lib/services/domain/ai-whatsapp/conversation-engine.service.ts exports handleIncomingWhatsApp()
lib/services/domain/ai-whatsapp/types.ts exports IncomingWhatsAppMessage, SessionExtractedData, ConversationMessage, ClaudeResponse
Session created on first message, reused within 2h window for same contact+team
Claude Haiku 4.5 called via generateText() + Output.object() with Zod schema (AI SDK 6.x pattern)
Structured output: { text, conversation_complete, extracted_data }
Fallback response if structured output fails
Photos: downloaded from Twilio MediaUrl0, stored in Supabase Storage documents bucket, base64 sent to Claude as multimodal image
Reply sent via sendWhatsAppMessage()
Session updated in DB after each message (messages array, extracted_data, status)
When conversation_complete=true, calls createInterventionFromSession()
npm run lint passes
userStories.notes: Session mgmt, Claude Haiku 4.5, Output.object(), photo handling, multimodal vision

userStories.title: Create intervention from session (3-step pattern + notifications)
userStories.description: As a gestionnaire, I want completed WhatsApp conversations to automatically create interventions with proper assignments and threads, so I see them in my dashboard.
acceptanceCriteria: lib/services/domain/ai-whatsapp/intervention-creator.service.ts exports createInterventionFromSession()
Uses InterventionRepository.create() with skipInitialSelect: true (RLS pattern)
Intervention fields: source='whatsapp_ai', creation_source='whatsapp_ai', created_by=identifiedUserId (nullable)
Step 2: Creates intervention_assignments for tenant (if identified) + ALL team gestionnaires
Step 3: Creates conversation_threads (group + tenant_to_managers) + conversation_participants
Tenant matched by phone via normalizePhoneE164 (reused from ai-phone)
Building matched by address (fuzzy best-effort)
Transcript saved in ai_phone_calls with channel='whatsapp_text', elevenlabs_conversation_id='wa-{sessionId}'
createInterventionNotification() called for push + email
Session updated with intervention_id and identified_user_id
npm run lint passes
userStories.notes: 3-step pattern: InterventionRepo.create + assignments + threads. normalizePhoneE164 reused.

userStories.title: Manual provisioning + lint validation
userStories.description: As a developer, I want documentation and a SQL seed for enabling WhatsApp on a test team, plus full lint validation of all new code.
acceptanceCriteria: DEV_WHATSAPP_PHONE_NUMBER documented in .env.example
SQL seed documented in plan for enabling WhatsApp on a team (UPDATE ai_phone_numbers SET whatsapp_enabled=true)
TWILIO_ACCOUNT_SID, TWILIO_AUTH_TOKEN, TWILIO_WEBHOOK_URL added to .env.example
npm run lint passes on ALL new files
No any types, no console.log in production code
